reason: The item tightly targets boundary-aware interpretation of variability (Texo vs delay time), conditional model corrections at high Texo, and the limits of “within-bounds” agreement—yielding meaningful expert discrimination. Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible (precision overclaim, direction reversal, mechanistic proof fallacy), though some options show mild stylistic/strength cues (e.g., “proves,” “exact,” “precise point estimate”) that can enable testwise elimination and slightly reduce discriminative strength.

The prompt cleanly targets two specific, boundary-sensitive mechanism explanations (venting-driven dT/dt decrease; heating-power-driven propagation delay via reduced heat-transfer duration), and it explicitly asks for why each is counterintuitive, which discourages superficial answers. Diagnostic value is high because it penalizes common but plausible misattributions (reaction completion; “more power means faster propagation”), though some ambiguity remains because Stage definitions and the propagation-interval reference time are not explicitly standardized in the prompt, which could admit alternative yet partially reasonable narratives.
